etymology.description: 留(留) ('to stay') + 土(土) ('ground') = to stay on the ground -> sit
etymology.interpretation: The character 坐 (zuò, 'sit; ride') combines the sense of 留 (liú, 'to stay') with 土 (tǔ, 'ground'), expressing the concept of remaining upon the ground, which naturally leads to the action of sitting. The idea of staying grounded naturally extends to 'sitting,' and by metaphor, to 'riding,' as one is seated while riding.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢在阳台上坐着读书(我喜歡在陽台上坐著讀書)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xǐhuān zài yángtái shàng zuòzhe dúshū.
examples.english: I enjoy sitting on the balcony to read.

examples.chinese: 我们可以一起坐火车去海边(我們可以一起坐火車去海邊)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen kěyǐ yīqǐ zuò huǒchē qù hǎibiān.
examples.english: We can take the train together to the seaside.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢坐在长椅上欣赏公园的风景(她喜歡坐在長椅上欣賞公園的風景)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zuò zài cháng yǐ shàng xīnshǎng gōngyuán de fēngjǐng.
examples.english: She likes sitting on the bench, enjoying the park's scenery.
